homepage.title: Emily Thornberry
homepage.text_excerpt: Emily Thornberry Skip to main content Emily Thornberry For Islington South and Finsbury Join Get Involved Donate About Emily Newsletter Your campaigns Foreign Policy Home Affairs Finance & Economy Nature & Environment Islington South and Finsbury Visits Events Councillors Contact Help with the cost of living Careers More Menu Emily Thornberry Prev Next Emily was born in Guildford in 1960. When she was seven her father left, and the family spent many years on benefits. A Labour councillor helped them find a house on a local council estate, and eventually persuaded Emily’s mum to run for the council herself. It was her mum’s inspiration that turned Emily into a lifelong Labour campaigner, and she has been a member of the party since her teenage years. She failed her 11-plus, went to a secondary modern, then moved to Hammersmith to complete her A-levels. Emily studied law at Kent University and took on variety of jobs to make ends meet, from working in a care home to cleaning toilets on cross-Channel ferries. After qualifying as a barrister in the mid-1980s, she began her career representing striking miners, Wapping print-workers and P&O seafarers, while continuing her Labour campaigning in East London, and marching against the rise of the National Front. She met her husband, Christopher, in law school, and they have three children. Emily first stood for election in Canterbury in 2001. She increased Labour’s vote share by 5.5 percentage points, turning the seat into a marginal and coming within 2000 votes of winning – Labour’s best result in Canterbury up to that point. When she next stood in Islington South and Finsbury in 2005, in the wake of the Iraq War, it was the top Lib Dem target seat in the country, but Emily personally knocked on 11,800 doors and won by 484 votes. Since first being elected in 2005, Emily has been re-elected five times. She has campaigned tirelessly on issues of great importance to the community of Islington South and Finsbury, including the housing crisis, the climate emergency, and social equality. She was one of the longest continually serving members of the shadow cabinet, and since 2016 has covered the Defence, Brexit, Foreign Affairs, International Trade, and Attorney General briefs. In September 2024, Emily was elected as the Chair of the Foreign Affairs Select Committee. The Committee scrutinises the Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office (FCDO) and UK foreign policy, investigates global challenges like disinformation, national security, and international relations. Emily Thornberry Contact details Rt Hon Emily Thornberry MP House of Commons London SW1A 0AA Rt Hon Emily Thornberry MP House of Commons London SW1A 0AA '> View on Google Maps Email: emily.thornberry.mp@parliament.uk Further details Published and promoted by Emily Thornberry MP, 65 Barnsbury Street, N1 1EJ ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved. pages.title: Your campaigns - Emily Thornberry
pages.text_excerpt: Your campaigns - Emily Thornberry Skip to main content Emily Thornberry For Islington South and Finsbury Join Get Involved Donate About Emily Newsletter Your campaigns Foreign Policy Home Affairs Finance & Economy Nature & Environment Islington South and Finsbury Visits Events Councillors Contact Help with the cost of living Careers More Menu Your campaigns Thank you to everyone who takes the time to write to me with your views and campaigns. I really enjoy reading your emails and endeavour to respond to everyone who emails me. If you’ve written to me about a policy issue, I receive thousands of mass campaign emails each week and I am therefore unable to respond to these directly. You can check out my views on current issues on this page. If your concerns are not fully addressed, please do get in touch directly with your thoughts. Your Campaigns Foreign Policy Home Affairs Nature & Environment Finance & Economy Emily Thornberry Contact details Rt Hon Emily Thornberry MP House of Commons London SW1A 0AA Rt Hon Emily Thornberry MP House of Commons London SW1A 0AA '> View on Google Maps Email: emily.thornberry.mp@parliament.uk Further details Published and promoted by Emily Thornberry MP, 65 Barnsbury Street, N1 1EJ ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved. pages.title: Contact - Emily Thornberry
pages.text_excerpt: Contact - Emily Thornberry Skip to main content Emily Thornberry For Islington South and Finsbury Join Get Involved Donate About Emily Newsletter Your campaigns Foreign Policy Home Affairs Finance & Economy Nature & Environment Islington South and Finsbury Visits Events Councillors Contact Help with the cost of living Careers More Menu Contact Meeting staff at the Whittington Maternity Unit. We hold two constituency surgeries a month, one by appointment and one walk in surgery. Our walk-in surgery is the second Saturday of every month, between 10am-12noon, at Islington Town Hall, Upper Street. Strict parliamentary protocol dictates that MPs are only able to assist with their own constituents’ cases. Please check that your postcode falls into the Islington South and Finsbury constituency before attending. You can do so here. If you want to make an appointment for our other surgery, please ring our advice line or send an email to emily.thornberry.mp@parliament.uk Emily Thornberry Contact details Rt Hon Emily Thornberry MP House of Commons London SW1A 0AA Rt Hon Emily Thornberry MP House of Commons London SW1A 0AA '> View on Google Maps Email: emily.thornberry.mp@parliament.uk Further details Published and promoted by Emily Thornberry MP, 65 Barnsbury Street, N1 1EJ ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved.
